title: Berlin Wall Memorial — Quick Summary
items: **The Berlin Wall Memorial on Bernauer Strasse is free, outdoors, and open every day.** The 1.4 km of memorial grounds are accessible daily from 08:00 to 22:00, and admission everywhere on the site is free.

**The indoor parts keep shorter hours and close on Mondays.** The Documentation Center, Visitor Center and rooftop viewing tower open Tuesday to Sunday, 10:00 to 18:00, with the last tower climb at 17:45. The open-air strip stays open even when they are shut.

**This is the only place in Berlin where you see the whole border, not just a wall.** An 80 m section is kept intact between two steel walls: the outer wall, the raked sand death strip, the patrol road and a watchtower base.

**Get there through S Nordbahnhof.** Take S1, S2, S25 or S26 to Nordbahnhof, tram M10, or U8 Bernauer Strasse / U6 Naturkundemuseum. Start with the free ghost-station exhibition inside Nordbahnhof before you go up to the street.

**Give it about an hour and walk it in order.** A natural route runs Nordbahnhof, Visitor Center, preserved strip, viewing tower, Window of Remembrance, Chapel of Reconciliation, and the escape-tunnel markers near Schwedter Strasse.
**It is not Checkpoint Charlie.** This is where the Wall is explained honestly, on the ground where it stood, rather than sold as a photo backdrop.
